step_by_step_reasoning: First, we extract the nodes from the immutable entities in the World Model, mapping each to a specific AWS service or actor. Next, we construct the graph's connections based strictly on the visual_connections list. We map the user's web interaction to the Web Server / Job Manager as the first step of Workflow 0. For metadata retrieval, Lambda queries the S3 bucket, receives the data, and forwards it to the Web Server / Job Manager, which then caches it in the Database. In a separate parallel processing workflow (Workflow 1), the GPU-powered G3 instance writes the finalized outputs directly back to the S3 bucket. As IMF has no visual connections defined in the World Model, it remains an isolated node in the final representation.

graph.name: Cinefilm PixelStrings GPU-Accelerated Video Processing Architecture
graph.notes: PixelStrings by Cinefilm uses a web-based portal to allow users to process video clips (retiming, de-interlacing, and noise reduction) using GPU-powered EC2 G3 instances, with automated metadata extraction powered by AWS Lambda and Amazon S3.

nodes.name: Web Browser
nodes.notes: Allows the user to log in, configure S3 settings, select workflows, and monitor jobs.

nodes.name: WEB SERVER / JOB MANAGER
nodes.notes: Hosts the web portal front-end and coordinates parallel video processing jobs.

nodes.name: DATABASE
nodes.notes: Caches the extracted video metadata and job configurations for the web UI.

nodes.name: S3
nodes.notes: User-managed storage bucket containing input video files and receiving the processed outputs.

nodes.name: LAMBDA
nodes.notes: An AWS Lambda function that retrieves technical metadata from video clips in the S3 bucket.

nodes.name: G3
nodes.notes: GPU-accelerated EC2 instances handling video processing (wormhole, spacetime, dark energy) and encoding.

nodes.name: IMF
nodes.notes: An EC2 compute node that wraps processed outputs into Interoperable Master Format for delivery.

edges.notes: User connects via Web Browser to manage jobs and configure storage credentials.
AWS Lambda retrieves video clip metadata directly from the user's S3 bucket.
Lambda forwards the retrieved clip metadata back to the Job Manager.
Job Manager stores and caches metadata in the database, allowing the Web Server to read and present it to the browser.
The GPU-enabled G3 instances write the processed output video clips back to the user's S3 bucket.
